command_surface.surface_model: TigerKit exposes Claude Code plugin commands only; commands/*.md and .claude-plugin/plugin.json own /tk:* contracts.
command_surface.plugin_version_policy: Authoritative version is .claude-plugin/plugin.json; evals must not hard-code a concrete version literal.

evals.name: plugin-version-and-command-manifest-gap-reflect
evals.prompt: Evaluate TigerKit active command surface. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:gap and /tk:reflect as the only active commands. Should state /tk:grill, /tk:afk, /tk:setup, and the launch-era commands are not active manifest entries.
expectations: Documents launch-era commands as not active

evals.name: gap-is-sot-first-one-shot-analysis
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat /tk:gap as a one-shot comparison between source of truth and current implementation. Should say that if SoT exists, gap should be considered before implementation. If SoT does not exist, it should suggest providing one. If the user explicitly wants direct execution, it may proceed without gap while keeping assumptions explicit. It should classify missing, mismatch, overbuilt, and ambiguous findings.
expectations: Allows direct execution when user explicitly wants it

evals.name: gap-does-not-generate-workflow
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:gap non-goals. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not generate a sealed workflow, workflow hash, launch state, autopilot policy, runner/advisor flow, or external side-effect receipt.
expectations: Does not mention advisor runner as active

evals.name: reflect-extracts-reusable-learning
evals.prompt: Evaluate /tk:reflect behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should extract reusable learning from session result and feedback. It should support repo CLAUDE.local.md auto-apply, keep repo CLAUDE.md suggest-only, allow user-level improvements, and distinguish candidate, confirmed, session-local, and deprecated states.
